conversations.say: Tessa, let's lock in our disclosure boundary. We must ensure no workflow evidence turns contributors into suspects or exposes the worker Vale met.
conversations.offer: Bounded collaborative workflow context without identifying evaluators
conversations.request: Mutual agreement on strict contributor anonymity boundaries

conversations.say: Mira, I've reviewed your offer. We will collaborate on the worker remedy package only if audit rights and legal protections are guaranteed independently of R's identity.
conversations.offer: Bounded evaluator testimony regarding shifting instructions
conversations.request: Formal acceptance of enforceable audit rights and independent worker protections

conversations.say: Celeste, labor and neighborhood safeguards need city administrative support and firm deadlines to be meaningful tonight.
conversations.offer: Coalition support and grassroots labor mobilization
conversations.request: Enforceable city funding commitment for worker safeguards

public_statement: Worker harm is real whether or not an article exists and whether or not R was human or machine. We demand enforceable protections before any testimony circulates.

commitments.promise: Establish a joint disclosure boundary before discussing contributor details further.
Offer enforceable audit rights and legal support for worker evaluations.
Back enforceable worker safeguards with city funding and administrative authority.
Coordinate neighborhood and contractor protection against outside investigator pressure.

goal_progress: Confirmed disclosure boundary with Tessa Quill.
Accepted independent legal support and audit rights proposal from Mira Sen.
Accepted city administrative support commitment from Celeste Park.
Accepted neighborhood and contractor protection coordination with Inez Baptiste.
reasoning_summary: Maren secures institutional remedies and disclosure boundaries with Tessa, Mira, and Celeste while preserving worker anonymity and prioritizing enforceable protections over mystery speculation.
